nodes.name: Emmy Award Winners
nodes.description: Outstanding Drama, Comedy, and acting category winners at the Primetime Emmys

items.prompt: Which NBC sitcom about a Seattle radio psychiatrist won Outstanding Comedy Series a record-tying 5 consecutive years (1994–1998), the first series ever to achieve that streak?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Comedy Series 5 consecutive years: 1994, 1995, 1996, 1997, 1998
First series to win 5 straight Comedy trophies; Modern Family later tied the record (2010–2014)
Accumulated 37 total Emmy wins—the most for any comedy series
Spun off from Cheers (1993); starred Kelsey Grammer as Dr. Frasier Crane
Beat Seinfeld, Friends, and other heavyweights repeatedly during its streak
Created by David Angell, Peter Casey, and David Lee; ran 11 seasons on NBC (1993–2004)

items.prompt: Which HBO fantasy series based on George R.R. Martin's novels holds the record for most Emmy wins by a scripted series, with 59 total awards?
items.explanation:
59 total Emmy wins—the most for any scripted television series
Won Outstanding Drama Series 4 times (2015, 2016, 2018, 2019)
Based on George R.R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire novels; aired 2011–2019
Peter Dinklage won Outstanding Supporting Actor 4 times for Tyrion Lannister
Final season drew massive viewership but polarized critics and fans
Set the record for most Emmy wins in a single year by a series: 12 in 2015 and again in 2016
items.cardFront: Most Emmys for a scripted series (59)

items.prompt: Which actress won Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series 6 consecutive years (2012–2017) for playing Vice President Selina Meyer, the most wins for a single role in Emmy history?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series 6 consecutive years (2012–2017) for Veep
6 wins for the same role surpassed the previous record of 5, held by Candice Bergen (Murphy Brown)
Also won an Emmy for Seinfeld (Outstanding Supporting Actress, 1996)—total of 11 Primetime Emmy wins
Veep ran on HBO from 2012–2019; she announced her breast cancer diagnosis 11 days after her 2017 win; Veep was on hiatus during her treatment and she was not nominated in 2018
Her 6 consecutive wins for Selina Meyer is the longest unbroken streak in any lead acting category
Daughter of billionaire Gérard Louis-Dreyfus; began career on Saturday Night Live (1982–1983)

items.prompt: Which actor won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series 4 times for playing a high school chemistry teacher turned drug lord, including 3 consecutive wins (2008–2010)?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series 4 times for Breaking Bad: 2008, 2009, 2010, and 2014
First actor in a cable series to win Lead Actor in a Drama 3 consecutive years
Played Walter White across 5 seasons on AMC (2008–2013)
Also won a Tony Award for Best Actor in a Play for Network (2019)
Before Breaking Bad, best known as Hal on Malcolm in the Middle
Joined only Dennis Franz (NYPD Blue, 4 wins) in quadruple Lead Actor Drama victories
items.cardFront: 4 Lead Actor Drama Emmys for Walter White

items.prompt: Which NBC political drama created by Aaron Sorkin won Outstanding Drama Series 4 consecutive years (2000–2003), tying the record held by Hill Street Blues and L.A. Law?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Drama Series 4 consecutive years: 2000, 2001, 2002, 2003
Tied with Hill Street Blues, L.A. Law, Mad Men, and Game of Thrones for most Drama Series wins
Created by Aaron Sorkin; Sorkin departed after season 4 and the show never won Drama again
Martin Sheen starred as President Josiah Bartlet but never won Outstanding Lead Actor despite 6 nominations
Won 9 Emmys at the 2000 ceremony—the most by any series in a single year at that time
Ran 7 seasons on NBC (1999–2006); 26 total Emmy wins

items.prompt: Which ABC mockumentary-style sitcom about three related families in Los Angeles won Outstanding Comedy Series 5 consecutive years (2010–2014), tying a record?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Comedy Series 5 consecutive years: 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014
Tied Frasier's record of 5 consecutive Comedy Series wins
Mockumentary format following three branches of the Pritchett-Dunphy-Tucker family
Created by Christopher Lloyd and Steven Levitan; aired on ABC 2009–2020
Ed O'Neill, Sofia Vergara, Ty Burrell, and Jesse Tyler Ferguson led the ensemble
Faced growing criticism for its dominance—many felt it blocked more deserving comedies like Parks and Recreation and Louie

items.prompt: Which Pop TV comedy about a wealthy family who loses everything and moves to a small town swept all 7 major comedy categories at the 2020 Emmys—the first series ever to do so?
items.explanation:
Swept all 7 major comedy categories at the 72nd Emmys (2020): Series, Lead Actor, Lead Actress, Supporting Actor, Supporting Actress, Writing, Directing
First series in Emmy history to sweep every major category in its genre
Eugene Levy and Catherine O'Hara won Lead Actor and Lead Actress; Daniel Levy won Writing, Directing, and Supporting Actor
Canadian production that aired on CBC in Canada and Pop TV in the U.S.
Created by Eugene Levy and Daniel Levy (father and son)
Ran 6 seasons (2015–2020); the sweep came for its final season
items.cardFront: Swept all 7 comedy categories at 2020 Emmys

items.prompt: Which NBC late-night sketch comedy show, on the air since 1975, holds the record for most Emmy wins of any program in television history, with over 100 total?
items.explanation:
Most Emmy wins of any television program in history—surpassed 100 total wins in 2024
Premiered October 11, 1975; created by Lorne Michaels, who has produced nearly every season
Won its 50th-season Emmys including Outstanding Variety Sketch Series
SNL50: The Anniversary Special (2025) won 8 additional Emmys including Outstanding Variety Special
Launched the careers of Eddie Murphy, Tina Fey, Will Ferrell, Bill Murray, and dozens more
Airs live from Studio 8H at 30 Rockefeller Plaza in New York City
items.cardFront: Most Emmy wins of any TV program (100+)

items.prompt: Which actress won Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series 4 consecutive years (1996–1999) for playing a documentary filmmaker married to Paul Reiser's character on an NBC sitcom?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series 4 consecutive years: 1996, 1997, 1998, 1999
Played Jamie Buchman on Mad About You (NBC, 1992–1999)
Also won the Academy Award for Best Actress for As Good as It Gets (1997)—won Emmy and Oscar in the same year
Her 4 consecutive wins were a record at the time, later surpassed by Julia Louis-Dreyfus (6 consecutive)
Mad About You depicted married life in New York with Paul Reiser; ran 7 seasons
Briefly revived Mad About You for a limited series on Spectrum Originals in 2019

items.prompt: Which Amazon comedy set in late-1950s New York about a housewife who discovers a talent for stand-up comedy won Outstanding Comedy Series at the 2018 Emmys?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Comedy Series in 2018 for its debut season
Rachel Brosnahan won Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series in 2018
Created by Amy Sherman-Palladino, who became the first woman to win both Directing and Writing Emmys for comedy in the same year
Set in late-1950s New York City; followed Miriam "Midge" Maisel's journey into stand-up comedy
Streamed on Amazon Prime Video; ran 5 seasons (2017–2023); accumulated 20 total Emmy wins
Alex Borstein won Outstanding Supporting Actress twice (2019, 2021) for Susie Myerson

items.prompt: Which actor was nominated for Emmy Awards for playing the same character on three different NBC series—a 1980s bar sitcom, a 1990s spinoff, and a guest spot on a third show—and won 4 times?
items.explanation:
First actor nominated for the same character on three different series: Cheers, Frasier, and Wings
Won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Comedy Series 4 times for Frasier: 1994, 1995, 1998, 2004
Played Dr. Frasier Crane for over 20 years across Cheers (1984–1993) and Frasier (1993–2004, revived 2023)
Received 10 Emmy nominations for the role of Frasier Crane alone
Also a prolific voice actor: Sideshow Bob on The Simpsons since 1990
Born Allen Kelsey Grammer in the U.S. Virgin Islands; overcame significant personal tragedies including the murders of his father and sister
items.cardFront: Emmy-nominated for same role on 3 shows

items.prompt: Which Apple TV+ comedy about an American football coach hired to manage an English soccer team won Outstanding Comedy Series in both its first and second seasons (2021–2022)?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Comedy Series in 2021 and 2022 for its first two seasons
Jason Sudeikis won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Comedy Series in both 2021 and 2022
Based on a character Sudeikis created for NBC Sports Premier League promos in 2013
First Apple TV+ series to win Outstanding Comedy Series; made streaming history
Brett Goldstein won Outstanding Supporting Actor twice (2021, 2022) for Roy Kent
Ran 3 seasons (2020–2023); won 11 total Emmy Awards
items.cardFront: Apple TV+ comedy, back-to-back Comedy wins

items.prompt: Which actor won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series 3 times (2000, 2001, 2003) for playing a New Jersey mob boss juggling organized crime and sessions with his psychiatrist?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series 3 times: 2000, 2001, 2003
Played Tony Soprano on HBO's The Sopranos (1999–2007), the role that defined prestige TV's antihero era
His portrayal of a mobster in therapy was ranked as the greatest TV character by multiple publications
Died of a heart attack in Rome on June 19, 2013, at age 51
The Sopranos won 21 total Emmys including 2 for Outstanding Drama Series
His son Michael Gandolfini played young Tony in the prequel film The Many Saints of Newark (2021)
items.cardFront: 3 Lead Actor Drama Emmys for Tony Soprano

items.prompt: Which CBS sitcom (1970–1977) about a single woman working in a Minneapolis TV newsroom won Outstanding Comedy Series 3 times in a row (1975–1977)?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Comedy Series 3 consecutive years: 1975, 1976, 1977 (its final three seasons)
Accumulated 29 Emmy wins—a record for a comedy until Frasier surpassed it in 2002
Groundbreaking portrayal of a single, independent working woman as a lead character
Spawned multiple spinoffs: Rhoda, Phyllis, and Lou Grant
Ed Asner won 3 Supporting Actor Emmys and 2 Lead Actor Emmys for Lou Grant across the original and spinoff
Created by James L. Brooks and Allan Burns; MTM Enterprises became a major TV production company

items.prompt: Which HBO drama about a dysfunctional media dynasty fighting over control of their father's global conglomerate won Outstanding Drama Series 3 times (2020, 2022, 2023)?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Drama Series 3 times: 2020 (season 2), 2022 (season 3), 2023 (season 4)
Created by Jesse Armstrong; ran 4 seasons on HBO (2018–2023)
Kieran Culkin won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series for the final season (2023)
Matthew Macfadyen won Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Drama Series (2022) for Tom Wambsgans
Inspired by real media families including the Murdochs; filmed largely in New York
The Roy family—Logan, Kendall, Shiv, Roman, Connor—became one of TV's most discussed ensembles

items.prompt: Which AMC drama about a chemistry teacher's descent into the methamphetamine trade won Outstanding Drama Series in both 2013 and 2014 for its final season?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Drama Series in 2013 and 2014—the final season was split into two parts, each eligible separately
16 total Emmy wins across its run (2008–2013)
Bryan Cranston won 4 Lead Actor Emmys; Aaron Paul won 3 Supporting Actor Emmys for Jesse Pinkman
Created by Vince Gilligan; aired on AMC for 5 seasons, 62 episodes
Spawned the spinoff Better Call Saul (2015–2022) and the film El Camino (2019)
Often ranked alongside The Sopranos and The Wire as the greatest TV drama ever made

items.prompt: Which actress won Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series 5 times for playing a tough-talking TV journalist, then withdrew from future nominations after her fifth win?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series 5 times: 1989, 1990, 1992, 1994, 1995—all for Murphy Brown
After her 5th win, she voluntarily withdrew from future Emmy consideration for the role
Played Murphy Brown, an investigative journalist and news anchor, on CBS (1988–1998, revived 2018)
The show famously clashed with Vice President Dan Quayle, who criticized the character for having a child out of wedlock in 1992
Her record for most Lead Actress Comedy wins stood until Julia Louis-Dreyfus won her 6th in 2017
Daughter of ventriloquist Edgar Bergen; also known for film roles in Starting Over and Miss Congeniality

items.prompt: Which AMC period drama set in a 1960s New York advertising agency won Outstanding Drama Series 4 consecutive years (2008–2011), the first basic cable series to win the category?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Drama Series 4 consecutive years: 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011
First basic cable series ever to win Outstanding Drama Series
Created by Matthew Weiner; aired on AMC for 7 seasons (2007–2015)
Jon Hamm starred as Don Draper but did not win Outstanding Lead Actor until 2015, his 7th and final nomination for the role
Tied with Hill Street Blues, L.A. Law, The West Wing, and Game of Thrones for most Drama Series wins (4)
Revived cultural interest in 1960s fashion, cocktail culture, and mid-century design

items.prompt: Which actor won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Comedy Series 4 times for playing a bigoted but lovable Queens dockworker, and later became the only man to win lead acting Emmys in both comedy and drama?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Comedy Series 4 times: 1972, 1977, 1978, 1979—all for All in the Family
Played Archie Bunker, one of the most iconic and controversial characters in TV history
All in the Family aired on CBS (1971–1979) and was the #1 rated show for 5 consecutive seasons
Also won Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series for In the Heat of the Night (1989)—making him the only male actor to win lead Emmys in both genres
The show tackled racism, sexism, and politics through Archie's arguments with son-in-law Mike "Meathead" Stivic (Rob Reiner)
Born in Manhattan; trained at the National University of Ireland in Dublin before pursuing acting

items.prompt: Which CBS sitcom (1971–1979) was the first show to top the Nielsen ratings for 5 consecutive seasons and won Outstanding Comedy Series 4 times in the 1970s?
items.explanation:
Won Outstanding Comedy Series 4 times: 1971, 1972, 1973, 1978
#1 rated show on television for 5 consecutive seasons (1971–1976)—the first series to achieve this
Created by Norman Lear; adapted from the British sitcom Till Death Us Do Part
Revolutionized the sitcom by addressing racism, homosexuality, abortion, and the Vietnam War
Spawned spinoffs Maude, The Jeffersons, Good Times, and Archie Bunker's Place
Jean Stapleton won Outstanding Lead Actress twice for Edith Bunker; Rob Reiner won twice for Mike Stivic
items.cardFront: 4 Comedy Series Emmys, #1 show 5 years running
